slice: Jace Beleren pair — WAR-55 Jace's Triumph + ZNR-63 Jace, Mirror Mage

citations_union: Scryfall: WAR-55 record — oracle_text references 'a Jace planeswalker'; subject_known_ip: Jace Beleren; ip_verified: true
Scryfall: ZNR-63 record — type_line 'Legendary Planeswalker — Jace' (subtype reserved for printings of Jace Beleren); ip_verified: true
MTG Wiki: Jace Beleren — War of the Spark section confirms Ravnica/Immortal-Sun captivity beat; Zendikar Rising section confirms Nahiri-pursuit / Murasa-Skyclave arc

divergences.opus_value: [] (parallel Sonnet pass landed the edit during Opus's spec read)
divergences.judge_note: Not a substantive divergence — race condition on file landing. Both agents proposed identical surgical edits to ZNR-63 (reciprocal Connections bullet); Sonnet's hand reached the file first. Opus self-documents the convergence in its sidecar's files_modified_note. Outside scope of the judge's verdict on the proposed move.

divergences.opus_value: Surfaces three: Mr. Nodeley character-node near-miss at N=2 with explicit N>=3 trigger threshold; WAR Triumph 5-card designer-confirmed-cycle; Jace's Defeat HOU-38 bookend.
divergences.sonnet_value: Surfaces three: WAR Triumph cycle; Defeat/Triumph bookend; Mirror Mage kicker-as-doppelganger tagging note.
divergences.judge_note: Both lists cover the same airspace — Triumph cycle, Defeat bookend, plus one model-specific extra. Opus's Mr. Nodeley near-miss is more load-bearing for future passes (defines the refactor inflection point); Sonnet's kicker-mechanic note is a sharper one-card observation. Complementary, not contradictory. Not a divergence in the proposal.

recommended_implementation: The Sonnet-landed edit on cards/magic-the-gathering/zendikar-rising/63-jace-mirror-mage.md is the converged proposal. Parent should verify the ZNR-63 Connections bullet on disk matches both sidecars' intent (reciprocal mirror back to WAR-55, two-acts-of-one-storyline framing). No further edits required on either card this pass.
judge_notes: Both proposals match on shape (1:1), topology (mirror), endpoints (WAR-55 and ZNR-63 in the same order), category (named-character-identity), and mood (sighed-but-mirrored). Citation chains overlap on the two Scryfall records and the MTG Wiki Jace Beleren entry — same underlying facts, identical phrasing on the wiki cite. The only difference of substance is which agent's hand reached the disk first; Sonnet landed the ZNR-63 Connections bullet while Opus was still reading the spec, and Opus's sidecar acknowledges the race and confirms structural identity of the would-have-written edit. The auxiliary observations (Triumph cycle, Defeat bookend) appear in both other_candidates_considered lists with the same deferral reasoning. Opus additionally flags the Mr. Nodeley character-node inflection point at N>=3 Jace cards — worth keeping on file for the next pass but not material to this verdict. Commit.
